content:
THURSTON COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE INCIDENT REPORT
Year-Case Number: 20-004433 Deputy: TURPIN ID Number: 1P75 Date: 9/3/20 Person(s)/Items Entered WACIC: NCIC: Signed Stolen Attached: List other documents attached reference case:
Narrative:
On 9/3/20 I was on uniform patrol for the Thurston County Sheriff's Office in a marked patrol vehicle. At around 1854 Hrs TCOMM TAC-1 advised of a disturbance with a gun at 7601 3RD Way SE which is the Tanglewild Townhomes.
At around 1854 Hrs TCOMM advised that the hysterical reporting party said someone opened fire and a female is screaming. Another caller from unit 11 advised, people are running all over and heard 5-6 shots. They advised that there were about 10 people outside.
TCOMM continued to update us with more information from multiple callers. The caller advised that they did not know how many people were shot, and did not see anyone down.
Another reporting party advised that several people have guns and are all men between the ages of 20-30 years old.
TCOMM then advised for 3S11 at 1855 Hrs, Sgt Seig from the Lacey Police Department, that the US Marshalls called him saying they were going to be out on 3RD Way SE looking for a suspect involved in a shooting in Portland, Or.
Another caller was yelling at TCOMM call taker, refusing to answer any questions but advised that she may see ID's on their back.
At around 1857 Hrs TCOMM advised PER Pierce County Sheriff's Office they have units on scene saying that they only need Aid.
At around 1858 Hrs Deputy Anderson and myself arrived on scene and saw US Marshalls and Pierce County Sheriff's Office on scene. All Marshall's and Deputies on scene were clearly marked as so, either on their outer carrier vest
